q: The boys (listen) to those songs for more than an hour.
a: The boys have been listening to those songs for more than an hour.

q: They both of them (play) football with their friends.
a: Both of them have been playing football with their friends.

q: Min Min (stare) at the letter for quite some time. I wonder what's wrong.
a: Min Min has been staring at the letter for quite some time. I wonder what's wrong.

q: The old woman (live) in this neighbourhood for the last eight years.
a: The old woman has been living in this neighbourhood for the last eight years.

q: We (look) for a new apartment since January.
a: We have been looking for a new apartment since January.

q: Those two women (talk) about setting up a joint-venture since yesterday.
a: Those two women have been talking about setting up a joint-venture since yesterday.

q: I (try) to contact you all day but no one knows where you went.
a: I have been trying to contact you all day but no one knows where you went.

q: Thae Thae (write) to her pen-friend for years.
a: Thae Thae has been writing to her pen-friend for years.

q: (How long / you / wait) for the bus before it finally arrived?
a: How long have you been waiting for the bus before it finally arrived?

q: The girls (learn) French since January.
a: The girls have been learning French since January.

q: Your mother is angry with you because you (watch) television all day.
a: Your mother is angry with you because you have been watching television all day.

q: U Hla Myint (teach) in this school for twelve years.
a: U Hla Myint has been teaching in this school for twelve years.

q: "He (think) about getting another job," I said.
a: "He has been thinking about getting another job," I said.

q: Where have you been? I (look) for you since this morning.
a: Where have you been? I have been looking for you since this morning.

q: Your eyes are red. You (cry).
a: Your eyes are red. You have been crying.

q: We (expect) him to turn up since four o'clock.
a: We have been expecting him to turn up since four o'clock.

q: We (work) very hard the whole day.
a: We have been working very hard the whole day.

q: Thank you so much for the camera. I (want) one for ages.
a: Thank you so much for the camera. I have been wanting one for ages.

q: She (not sleep) well lately because of stress at work.
a: She hasn't been sleeping well lately because of stress at work.

q: The girls (save) money to go on a holiday.
a: The girls have been saving money to go on a holiday.

q: The typist (type) letters for the last half hour.
a: The typist has been typing letters for the last half hour.

q: I (feel) nauseous since this morning.
a: I have been feeling nauseous since this morning.

q: Do you know that U Zaw Aung and his wife (travel) round Europe for a month?
a: Do you know that U Zaw Aung and his wife have been travelling round Europe for a month?

q: I (collect) badges since I was six years old.
a: I have been collecting badges since I was six years old.

q: There is paint on his clothes. He (paint) the room.
a: There is paint on his clothes. He has been painting the room.

q: Do you see that man? He (stand) at the doorway for the last fifteen minutes.
a: Do you see that man? He has been standing at the doorway for the last fifteen minutes.

q: The workmen have been (repair) the road for two days.
a: The workmen have been repairing the road for two days.

q: That team (win) the championship for three consecutive years.
a: That team has been winning the championship for three consecutive years.

q: Your clothes are dirty. What (you do)?
a: Your clothes are dirty. What have you been doing?

q: I (study) for my final exams all week, and I'm really tired now.
a: I have been studying for my final exams all week, and I'm really tired now.

q: He (practice) the guitar every evening after school.
a: He has been practicing the guitar every evening after school.

q: Cindy and Kathy (shop) since this morning.
a: Cindy and Kathy have been shopping since this morning.

q: Zaw Htet (wait) for you for the past one hour.
a: Zaw Htet has been waiting for you for the past one hour.

q: (	_ you run)? You are out of breath.
a: Have you been running? You are out of breath.

q: "How long (you sell) insurance?" asked Daw Tin Hla.
a: "How long have you been selling insurance?" asked Daw Tin Hla.
